commit.message:
chore(devcontainer): run real openclaw gateway with staging-parity config init (#183)

* chore(devcontainer): run real openclaw gateway with staging-parity config init

Turns the devcontainer into a real, runnable openclaw gateway instead of
a passive shell, mirroring how a bot pod is configured in staging.

- openclaw.json.tmpl: full fastclaw helm defaultConfigs["2026.4.2"] merged
  with the zooclaw _bot_lifecycle.py:create_bot db patch (5 LiteLLM models,
  all plugins, diagnostics-otel, zooguardian, tool denylist, gateway hot
  reload, etc. — no simplification, drift-trackable against gcp-foundation)
- postCreateCommand.sh: envsubst-render template → ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json
  → openclaw config validate (with doctor --fix fallback per start-gateway.sh)
  → background `openclaw gateway --port 18789 --bind lan --allow-unconfigured
  --dev`. GATEWAY_TOKEN auto-generated and persisted to ~/.openclaw/.gateway-token
- docker-compose.yml: port 8000→18789; pass through all 31 env vars from
  the 3 staging layers (helm pod_default + zooclaw per-bot + bot identity);
  *_BASE_URL defaults derived from $LITELLM_API_BASE, *_API_KEY defaults
  derived from $LITELLM_API_KEY to minimize required dev fields
- .env.example: documents all 3 layers with staging public URLs as
  reference defaults; secrets/keys left blank

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(devcontainer): pull bot env from fastclaw admin API instead of hand-listing

Previous .env.example forced the dev to hand-fill 30+ vars mirroring
staging vault-openclaw-env-secret + zooclaw bot-create injection.
Replaces all of that with a 2-var config (BOT_ID + FASTCLAW_ADMIN_TOKEN)
that postCreate hits to fetch the bot's deployment.env at boot.

- postCreateCommand.sh: curl GET /bot/api/v1/admin/bots/$BOT_ID with the
  fastclaw admin token (bypasses per-app ownership check, single shared
  token instead of per-user app tokens), jq @sh-encode each
  bot.config.deployment.env entry into ~/.openclaw/env.sh, source it +
  patch it into ~/.bashrc + ~/.zshrc so all later VSCode terminals see
  the same vars (USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN / AGENT_IDENTITY_* / LITELLM_API_KEY
  / NANGO_GATEWAY_URL / etc). LITELLM_API_BASE in .env still wins because
  the pod's value is cluster-internal and unreachable from a dev machine.
- docker-compose.yml: shrink env block from 31 vars to 8; default
  FASTCLAW_BASE_URL=https://claw.yesy.live and
  LITELLM_API_BASE=https://litellm.vllm.yesy.live so devs don't need to
  override the URLs.
- .env.example: shrinks to BOT_ID + FASTCLAW_ADMIN_TOKEN (required) plus
  three optional overrides; instructions point at ~/.fastclaw/config.json.staging
  for the admin token (matches what fastclaw-cli already uses).
- openclaw.json.tmpl: kept as-is — pod's runtime/config can't be reused
  because it embeds cluster-internal URLs that don't resolve from the dev
  machine.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(devcontainer): bump openclaw-docker image 2026.4.2.12 → 2026.4.2.19

Same 2026.4.2 schema series — defaultConfigs["2026.4.2"] in
.devcontainer/openclaw.json.tmpl remains valid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(devcontainer): move config rendering host-side, slim postCreate

The previous design did everything inside the container (postCreate would
curl fastclaw, render openclaw.json, validate, then start the gateway).
Restructure so config preparation happens BEFORE the container starts:

- initializeCommand.sh (host) now does all the heavy lifting in 5 numbered
  steps: validate .env, fetch bot from fastclaw admin API, render
  .devcontainer/openclaw.env (compose env_file) and .devcontainer/openclaw.json
  (bind-mounted into container), pull image, host fs setup. The fetched
  deployment.env is also rewritten to swap cluster-internal LiteLLM URL
  (http://litellm.openclaw.internal) for the public host so the gateway
  can actually reach it from a dev machine.
- docker-compose.yml uses env_file: openclaw.env to pipe all 17 vars
  into the container env at start (USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN, AGENT_IDENTITY_*,
  LITELLM_API_KEY, BOT_TOKEN, etc.) and bind-mounts openclaw.json.
- postCreateCommand.sh shrinks from 220 lines to ~100: just sanity-check
  the bind-mount, openclaw config validate (with doctor --fix fallback),
  start gateway, poll /healthz. No more curl/jq/envsubst/rcfile-patching
  inside the container.
- .gitignore: ignore the three host-generated files (openclaw.env contains
  every API key, openclaw.json embeds them, .gateway-token is the bot's
  shared secret).
- Bonus fixes from earlier rounds: trailing-newline guarantee in upsert,
  fused-key corruption detection only on script-managed key names (no
  longer false-flags base64 padding =), `version: "3.8"` removed,
  container_name dropped to let compose auto-name.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(devcontainer): use config dir not package dir for OPENCLAW_HOME, surface webchat URL

- Image's Dockerfile sets OPENCLAW_HOME to the npm package install dir
  (/usr/local/lib/node_modules/openclaw); the config + workspace dir is
  the symlink target /home/node/.openclaw. The image's own init.sh hard-
  overrides this for the same reason — postCreate must too, otherwise
  the bind-mounted openclaw.json is not found.
- Add webchat URL to the "How to interact" block. Pattern matches what
  fastclaw bot_connect.go:78 generates: <base>/#token=<token>. For dev
  that's http://localhost:18789/#token=$GATEWAY_TOKEN.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(devcontainer): simplify post-setup message

Replace the per-step list (already shown by postCreate) with a brief
"~1 minute, watch the Dev Containers panel" hint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(devcontainer): split cluster-wide env into committed openclaw-default.env

The fastclaw admin API only returns per-bot db deployment.env, which
doesn't include cluster-wide pod_default vars (LITELLM_API_BASE,
ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, GEMINI_PROJECT_ID, etc.) — those come from helm
defaultDeployment.env. Without them the gateway boots but skills can't
reach LiteLLM (LITELLM_API_BASE missing) and Vertex (GEMINI_* missing).

- New committed file .devcontainer/openclaw-default.env mirrors
  gcp-foundation/.../openclaw-fastclaw/values.yaml defaultDeployment.env,
  with cluster-internal LiteLLM URL (http://litellm.openclaw.internal)
  rewritten to public (https://litellm.vllm.yesy.live).
- docker-compose.yml loads two env_file in order: openclaw-default.env
  first (committed defaults), openclaw.env second (per-bot, generated).
  compose-spec semantics: last-listed file wins on duplicate keys, so
  per-bot values override defaults where they collide.
- initializeCommand.sh's openclaw.env writer no longer needs to
  inline-emit the helm defaults — that's openclaw-default.env's job.

Drift risk: openclaw-default.env must be kept in sync with
gcp-foundation values.yaml. Recommend a scheduled monthly diff.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(devcontainer): split postCreate/postStart, fix gateway lifecycle, branding

End-to-end verified after multiple rebuild cycles. The devcontainer now
boots cleanly, openclaw gateway survives across `docker restart`, and
host can hit http://localhost:18789 (webchat) directly.

Lifecycle restructure (the meat):
- New postStartCommand.sh runs on every container start (initial create
  AND every `docker restart`), so a crashed/restarted gateway recovers.
  postCreateCommand drops gateway start and keeps only the one-time
  init: chown /home/node/.openclaw (docker bind-mount flips it to root,
  blocks node user mkdir), recreate state dirs (.codex/.acpx/.agents),
  symlink ~/.agents, run /init-agent-workspace.sh main, validate config.
- Override image ENTRYPOINT to ["sleep", "infinity"]. Image's own
  init.sh fails as node user against root-owned /home/node/.openclaw
  (chown only happens in postCreate, but ENTRYPOINT runs first), so
  let postCreate own all init.
- Add `init: true` to compose so tini reaps zombies — without it,
  crashed gateway processes leave [openclaw] <defunct> entries.
- Use `setsid openclaw gateway run` (not bare `openclaw gateway`,
  which is the systemd service installer; not just `nohup`, which
  doesn't fully detach — gateway dies ~5s after postStart returns due
  to SIGHUP propagation through Cursor's exec wrapper).

Bind mounts:
- /workspace mounted from .devcontainer/.workspace (host bind, not
  named volume — uid mapping preserved for node-user mkdir; named
  volume initializes as root-owned and breaks init-agent-workspace.sh).
  Pre-create with .gitkeep so first rebuild doesn't have to mkdir.

Branding & UX:
- "ecap-skills devcontainer" → "zooclaw devcontainer" (banner +
  devcontainer.json name + final ready message).
- workspaceFolder: .openclaw/skills → /home/node so terminals open in
  HOME (also avoids openclaw scanning workspace/.devcontainer/.env).
- forwardPorts removed; rely on compose `ports: 18789:18789` for
  direct host bind, with portsAttributes for VSCode label.
- Slim VSCode extensions list 14 → 2 (python + pylance only). Cursor
  has built-in AI so Copilot is redundant; jupyter/gitlens/docker/
  github-actions not needed in this repo. Cuts rebuild time noticeably.
- postCreate output structured in numbered phases with green/yellow/
  red markers; initializeCommand tail has yellow boxed "PLEASE BE
  PATIENT — gateway boot ≈ 1 minute" banner; postStart prints big
  green "ZOOCLAW DEVCONTAINER IS READY" when /healthz returns 200.

Compose:
- Disable image's HEALTHCHECK (postStart owns the readiness signal —
  having both means redundant "(starting)" → "(unhealthy)" status that
  misleads when config is broken).

.gitignore:
- .devcontainer/.workspace/* (with !.gitkeep exception so the
  bind-mount target dir always exists).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(devcontainer): preserve openclaw.json edits across rebuilds

Previously initializeCommand.sh always re-rendered openclaw.json from
the template on every rebuild, wiping any models / channels / etc the
user had added directly to the file.

Skip render if the rendered openclaw.json already exists (and is
non-empty). Two escape hatches when a fresh render is actually wanted
(after rotating LITELLM_API_KEY upstream, changing the .tmpl, etc):

  rm .devcontainer/openclaw.json && rebuild
  # or
  OPENCLAW_FORCE_RERENDER=1 in .devcontainer/.env

Documented in .env.example.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(devcontainer): track rolling :2026.4.2 tag instead of pinning .19

openclaw-docker CI now publishes both :2026.4.2.X (immutable) and
:2026.4.2 (rolling minor track) on each tag push (see
openclaw-docker#59). Devcontainer pins the rolling one so devs pick
up new patches automatically — initializeCommand.sh already does
`docker pull --platform linux/amd64 "$IMAGE"` on every rebuild, so
GHCR digest comparison brings the latest down without manual bumps.

Production / staging stay on the immutable tag for reproducibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(devcontainer): clearer banners for postCreate vs postStart

postCreate's job is validation + one-time init; postStart's job is
starting the gateway. Make banners reflect that:

- postCreate header:  "postCreate" → "postCreate validation"
- postCreate footer:  "one-time init complete — gateway will start via
                       postStartCommand"
                       → "validation passed — postStartCommand starts
                       the gateway next"
- postStart header:   add matching "postStart: gateway" banner so the
                      Dev Containers panel reads as two clear sections.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(devcontainer): user guide for zooclaw devcontainer

Walks through prerequisites, first-time setup (clone → fill .env →
reopen in container), the 4-stage lifecycle (initializeCommand /
container / postCreate / postStart), common workflows (skill dev,
config edits with hot-reload, image upgrades, plugin/runtime dev),
file reference, and troubleshooting.

Also captures architecture decisions that get asked repeatedly:
ENTRYPOINT override, postCreate/postStart split, setsid for gateway
detach, host bind-mount workspace dir, rolling :2026.4.2 image tag.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(devcontainer): use <container> placeholder, drop hardcoded user name

Replace `ecap-skills-zhuguangbin-app-1` with the existing `<container>`
convention used elsewhere in the README. Add a short callout near the
top explaining how compose derives the actual name (`ecap-skills-$USER-
app-1`) so readers can substitute correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(devcontainer): route Claude models via anthropic provider in tmpl

Tested in container: Claude responses route correctly through LiteLLM's
/v1/messages endpoint. Native Anthropic-messages format gives better
tool-use semantics than openai-completions translation.

- claude-sonnet-4-6 + claude-haiku-4-5 moved from openai → anthropic
  provider (with full metadata: input/contextWindow/maxTokens/compat)
- anthropic provider gets api: anthropic-messages and auth: api-key
- agents.defaults: heartbeat / model.primary / compaction now reference
  anthropic/claude-*; pdfModel was already anthropic
- openai provider keeps non-Claude models (gpt-5.4, gemini-3-flash,
  kimi-k2.6) as fallbacks; memorySearch keeps openai/text-embedding-3
  (Claude doesn't do embeddings)

A matching gcp-foundation PR will follow to apply the same change to
defaultConfigs["2026.4.2"] across dev/staging/production.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -0,0 +1,269 @@
+# Zooclaw Devcontainer — User Guide
+
+A reproducible local environment for working on **our openclaw distribution** and the **skills/plugins** that ship with it. The devcontainer runs a real openclaw gateway (same image staging uses), with the per-bot config, env vars, and skills mounted from your repo.
+
+## What you get
+
+- A container running `ghcr.io/serendipityoneinc/openclaw-docker:2026.4.2` (rolling minor track — always the latest patch)
+- An openclaw gateway listening on `localhost:18789`, configured to mirror a staging bot's setup
+- Your `ecap-skills` repo mounted at `~/.openclaw/skills/` so changes you make are immediately picked up
+- Webchat at `http://localhost:18789/#token=<your-gateway-token>`
+- LiteLLM, ECAP proxy, agent identity, NangoGateway, and all the other staging URLs already wired in
+
+## Prerequisites
+
+| Tool | Why | Install |
+|------|-----|---------|
+| Docker (OrbStack recommended) | Run the container | https://orbstack.dev |
+| VSCode or Cursor + **Dev Containers** extension | Boot the devcontainer | `ms-vscode-remote.remote-containers` |
+| `gh` CLI (optional) | Auto-inject GitHub token into the container | `brew install gh && gh auth login` |
+| **Fastclaw admin token** | Pull your bot's per-deployment env from staging fastclaw | Ask the platform admin (shared staging secret) |
+| **A bot ID** in staging | The devcontainer's gateway is configured against this bot's identity | Look up at https://ecap.gensmo.nosay.live/en/claw-settings?tab=status |
+
+## First-time setup
+
+```bash
+# 1. Clone
+git clone git@github.com:SerendipityOneInc/ecap-skills.git
+cd ecap-skills
+
+# 2. Configure the two required secrets
+cp .devcontainer/.env.example .devcontainer/.env
+$EDITOR .devcontainer/.env
+#   BOT_ID=<your bot id>
+#   FASTCLAW_ADMIN_TOKEN=<admin token>
+
+# 3. Open in container
+#    VSCode/Cursor → Command Palette → "Dev Containers: Reopen in Container"
+```
+
+First boot takes **~3-5 minutes** (Cursor downloads its server + extensions, image is pulled, postCreate validates, postStart starts the gateway). Watch the **Dev Containers Output panel** for progress. When you see `✅ ZOOCLAW DEVCONTAINER IS READY`, the gateway is live.
+
+Subsequent rebuilds: ~30-60 seconds.
+
+> **`<container>` placeholder.** Examples below use `<container>` for the
+> running container name. Compose names it `ecap-skills-$USER-app-1`
+> (e.g. `ecap-skills-alice-app-1`). Look it up with `docker ps`, or
+> shell-substitute on the fly: `docker restart "ecap-skills-$USER-app-1"`.
+
+## Lifecycle
+
+```
+┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
+│ HOST                                                                     │
+│  initializeCommand.sh — runs before container starts                     │
+│   [1] Validate .env (BOT_ID + FASTCLAW_ADMIN_TOKEN)                      │
+│   [2] curl fastclaw → write .devcontainer/openclaw.env                   │
+│       (per-bot deployment.env: USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN / AGENT_IDENTITY_*    │
+│        / LITELLM_API_KEY / NANGO_GATEWAY_URL / etc.)                     │
+│   [3] Render openclaw.json from openclaw.json.tmpl                       │
+│       (skip if already exists — preserves your edits)                    │
+│   [4] docker pull (rolling tag picks up latest patch)                    │
+│   [5] mkdir ~/.claude (host bind-mount target)                           │
+└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
+                                    ▼
+┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
+│ CONTAINER                                                                │
+│  Image's ENTRYPOINT overridden → just `sleep infinity` (PID 1 = tini).   │
+│  Compose mounts:                                                         │
+│   • repo            → /home/node/.openclaw/skills                        │
+│   • openclaw.json   → /home/node/.openclaw/openclaw.json                 │
+│   • .workspace/     → /workspace                                         │
+│  Compose env_file (last-wins): openclaw-default.env + openclaw.env       │
+└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
+                                    ▼
+┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
+│ postCreateCommand.sh — runs ONCE after container creation                │
+│  [1/3] Environment check (versions)                                      │
+│  [2/3] Sanity check                                                      │
+│        - chown /home/node/.openclaw → node (docker bind-mount flips it)  │
+│        - mkdir state dirs (.codex, .acpx, .agents)                       │
+│        - /init-agent-workspace.sh main                                   │
+│  [3/3] openclaw config validate                                          │
+└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
+                                    ▼
+┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
+│ postStartCommand.sh — runs on EVERY container start (incl. restart)      │
+│   setsid openclaw gateway run --port 18789 --bind lan ...                │
+│   poll /healthz until ready (~10-30s)                                    │
+│   print webchat URL + log path                                           │
+└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
+```
+
+After this:
+- Gateway is at `http://localhost:18789`
+- Webchat at `http://localhost:18789/#token=<token>` (printed in postStart output, or `cat .devcontainer/.gateway-token` on host)
+- Logs: `tail -f ~/.openclaw/gateway.log` inside container
+- Container env vars: `env | grep -E '^(BOT_|LITELLM_|AGENT_|USER_|NANGO_|ECAP_|ZOOCLAW_)'`
+
+## Common workflows
+
+### Develop a skill
+
+```bash
+# Inside the container terminal
+cd ~/.openclaw/skills/<your-skill>
+# Edit SKILL.md / scripts. Changes are immediate — gateway picks them up
+# the next time the agent invokes the skill (no restart needed).
+```
+
+### Add a model / agent / channel to openclaw.json
+
+The rendered `openclaw.json` is **preserved across rebuilds** — initializeCommand only writes it if it doesn't exist. Edit either:
+- Host: `.devcontainer/openclaw.json` (bind-mounted into container)
+- Container: `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` (same file via the bind mount)
+
+Most fields hot-reload (`gateway.reload.mode = "hot"` is set). Look for `[gateway/reload]` in the gateway log to confirm. Fields that need a full gateway restart:
+
+| Field | Reason |
+|-------|--------|
+| `gateway.port` / `gateway.bind` | listener restart required |
+| `channels.*` | hot-reload bug in 4.2 (ECA-511; fixed in 4.15+) |
+| `plugins.entries.*.enabled` | plugin lifecycle changes |
+
+To restart **just the gateway** (faster than rebuilding the container):
+
+```bash
+docker restart <container>   # ~30s, runs postStart
+```
+
+### Force a fresh openclaw.json
+
+Default behavior preserves your edits. To reset to template:
+
+```bash
+# Option A: just delete it
+rm .devcontainer/openclaw.json
+# next rebuild re-renders from .tmpl
+
+# Option B: env flag
+echo 'OPENCLAW_FORCE_RERENDER=1' >> .devcontainer/.env
+# next rebuild re-renders. Remove the line afterwards.
+```
+
+When to do this: rotated `LITELLM_API_KEY` upstream, changed the `.tmpl`, bot config drifted, etc.
+
+### Pull the latest openclaw image
+
+The compose `image:` line tracks `:2026.4.2` (rolling minor track), so `initializeCommand.sh` does `docker pull` on every rebuild and picks up new patches automatically. No file changes needed.
+
+To upgrade to a different minor (e.g. 2026.4.15 once it's out):
+
+```yaml
+# .devcontainer/docker-compose.yml
+image: ghcr.io/serendipityoneinc/openclaw-docker:2026.4.15
+```
+
+### Develop a plugin
+
+Plugins are bundled into the openclaw-docker image — to test a new plugin you have two paths:
+
+1. **Quick local test**: `npm install -g @yourorg/your-plugin` inside the container, restart gateway. The plugin needs to be in `plugins.entries.<name>` of `openclaw.json` to load.
+2. **Bake it in**: send a PR to `openclaw-docker` to `npm pack` your plugin in the Dockerfile. Once that PR merges and an image tag is cut, devcontainer picks it up via the rolling tag.
+
+### Develop a feature on openclaw itself
+
+The openclaw runtime ships in the image at `/usr/local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/`. To test a local checkout:
+
+```bash
+# Inside container, override the package install with your fork:
+cd ~/openclaw && npm pack
+sudo npm install -g ./openclaw-*.tgz
+# Restart gateway
+docker restart <container>
+```
+
+For sustained dev: build a one-off image from your fork (`docker build -f Dockerfile.2026.4.2 ...`) and override the compose `image:` to point at it.
+
+## File reference
+
+| Path | Tracked? | Purpose |
+|------|----------|---------|
+| `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` | git | VSCode/Cursor entrypoint — declares hooks, mounts, extensions |
+| `.devcontainer/docker-compose.yml` | git | Compose service definition — image, ports, mounts, env_file |
+| `.devcontainer/openclaw.json.tmpl` | git | Config template (full `defaultConfigs["2026.4.2"]` from gcp-foundation + zooclaw bot-create overlay) |
+| `.devcontainer/openclaw-default.env` | git | Cluster-wide pod_default env (mirrors helm `defaultDeployment.env`, with internal LiteLLM URL rewritten to public) |
+| `.devcontainer/.env.example` | git | Template for the user's `.env` |
+| `.devcontainer/.workspace/.gitkeep` | git | Pre-create the `/workspace` bind-mount target |
+| `.devcontainer/initializeCommand.sh` | git | Host hook — validates env, fetches bot, renders config, pulls image |
+| `.devcontainer/postCreateCommand.sh` | git | Container hook (once) — chown / state dirs / config validate |
+| `.devcontainer/postStartCommand.sh` | git | Container hook (every start) — start gateway, poll healthz |
+| `.devcontainer/.env` | **gitignored** | Your secrets (BOT_ID, FASTCLAW_ADMIN_TOKEN) + compose meta |
+| `.devcontainer/openclaw.env` | **gitignored** | Per-bot deployment.env fetched from fastclaw |
+| `.devcontainer/openclaw.json` | **gitignored** | Rendered config (substituted env, your edits preserved across rebuilds) |
+| `.devcontainer/.gateway-token` | **gitignored** | Persistent shared-secret for webchat auth |
+
+## Configuration sources & precedence
+
+The container env is layered, last wins:
+
+1. `openclaw-default.env` (committed) — staging-wide URLs and IDs
+2. `openclaw.env` (generated per-bot) — per-bot secrets and identity
+3. `docker-compose.yml environment:` (compose) — proxy + dev-only knobs
+
+The openclaw config (`openclaw.json`) is rendered from `.tmpl` once and then your edits stick. The `.tmpl` itself is the merged result of:
+
+- gcp-foundation `defaultConfigs["2026.4.2"]` (every bot pod gets this)
+- zooclaw `_bot_lifecycle.py:create_bot` patch (apiKey/baseUrl override + memorySearch.remote)
+
+This mirrors the layering fastclaw does at pod startup time. The base template is intentionally complete — strip what you don't need by editing your local `openclaw.json`, or extend it.
+
+## Troubleshooting
+
+### `ERR_EMPTY_RESPONSE` on `localhost:18789`
+
+Gateway died or never started. Check:
+
+```bash
+docker exec <container> bash -c '
+  pgrep -af "openclaw gateway run"
+  curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:18789/healthz
+  tail -20 ~/.openclaw/gateway.log
+'
+```
+
+Most likely:
+- Gateway crashed during plugin load → check the runtime log at `/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-<date>.log`
+- Container restarted but postStart didn't run → `docker restart <container>` to retrigger
+
+### `postCreate validation` exits with `Config file not found`
+
+`OPENCLAW_HOME` got overridden somewhere — the image's symlink resolution doubles the path. Don't `export OPENCLAW_HOME=...` yourself; rely on the Dockerfile default.
+
+### `unauthorized: too many failed authentication attempts`
+
+The gateway's in-memory rate limiter tripped. `docker restart <container>` clears it.
+
+### initializeCommand fails: `fastclaw returned HTTP 404`
+
+Your `BOT_ID` in `.env` is wrong, or the bot was deleted. Look up the current ID at https://ecap.gensmo.nosay.live/en/claw-settings?tab=status.
+
+### postCreate fails: `BOT_ID value looks fused with another key`
+
+You hand-edited `.devcontainer/.env` without a trailing newline, and the script-managed appends fused onto your line. Open `.env`, put each `KEY=value` on its own line, save with a final newline, rebuild.
+
+### Cursor stuck downloading `cursor-reh-linux-x64.tar.gz`
+
+First-time setup downloads the Cursor server (~150-200MB) inside the container. Slow networks can take 2-5 minutes. Cached on subsequent rebuilds. If genuinely stuck, check container's network access (`docker exec <container> curl -I https://downloads.cursor.com/`).
+
+### `/home/node/.openclaw is owned by root`
+
+Docker bind-mounting child paths under `/home/node/.openclaw/` flips the parent dir to root ownership. postCreate's chown step should fix this — if it didn't run, do it manually:
+
+```bash
+docker exec <container> sudo chown node:node /home/node/.openclaw
+```
+
+## Architecture decisions
+
+These get asked enough to bake them in:
+
+- **Why override the image's ENTRYPOINT?** Image's `init.sh` runs as node user, but docker bind-mount flips `/home/node/.openclaw/` to root, so init's mkdir of state dirs fails with EACCES. We override entrypoint to plain `sleep infinity`, then postCreate (which has sudo) does chown + recreates the state dirs init.sh would have made.
+
+- **Why split postCreate / postStart?** postCreate runs once per container; postStart runs on every restart. We want gateway to come back automatically after `docker restart`, so gateway start lives in postStart. Validation / dir setup is once-only, so it's in postCreate.
+
+- **Why `setsid`, not just `nohup &`?** Cursor's exec wrapper for hooks propagates SIGHUP differently. `nohup &` left the gateway dying ~5s after postStart returned. `setsid` puts it in a new session with no controlling terminal, fully detached.
+
+- **Why a custom workspace bind-mount instead of a named volume?** Named volumes init as root-owned in OrbStack, breaking `init-agent-workspace.sh`'s mkdir. A host bind-mount (`./.workspace`) inherits host uid mapping, which matches node (uid 1000) cleanly.
+
+- **Why a rolling `:2026.4.2` tag?** Devs shouldn't have to edit `docker-compose.yml` every patch. The openclaw-docker CI publishes both `:2026.4.2.X` (immutable) and `:2026.4.2` (rolling) on each release. Production / staging keeps the immutable; only dev tooling uses the rolling tag. See [openclaw-docker#59](https://github.com/SerendipityOneInc/openclaw-docker/pull/59).

+#!/bin/bash
+# Runs every time the container starts (initial create + every restart).
+# Starts openclaw gateway in the background, polls /healthz until ready,
+# prints the webchat URL.
+#
+# postCreate already did the one-time init (chown / state dirs / config
+# validate). On a plain `docker restart`, postCreate does NOT re-run, so
+# this script is what brings the gateway back up.
+set -e
+
+if [ -t 1 ]; then
+    BOLD=$'\033[1m'; OK=$'\033[32m'; WARN=$'\033[33m'; ERR=$'\033[31m'; DIM=$'\033[2m'; RST=$'\033[0m'
+else
+    BOLD=''; OK=''; WARN=''; ERR=''; DIM=''; RST=''
+fi
+ok()   { printf '  %sok%s   %s\n' "$OK"   "$RST" "$1"; }
+warn() { printf '  %s!!%s   %s\n' "$WARN" "$RST" "$1"; }
+fail() { printf '  %sxx%s   %s\n' "$ERR"  "$RST" "$1" >&2; }
+
+CONFIG_DIR="/home/node/.openclaw"
+LOG="${CONFIG_DIR}/gateway.log"
+GATEWAY_PORT="${OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT:-18789}"
+
+printf '\n%s=== zooclaw devcontainer postStart: gateway ===%s\n' "$BOLD" "$RST"
+
+if [ "${OPENCLAW_AUTOSTART:-1}" != "1" ]; then
+    warn "OPENCLAW_AUTOSTART=0 — skipping gateway start"
+    echo "  manual:  openclaw gateway run --port ${GATEWAY_PORT} --bind lan --allow-unconfigured --dev"
+    exit 0
+fi
+
+# If a healthy gateway is already up (e.g. previous postStart still alive),
+# don't double-start.
+if curl -fsS -m 2 "http://127.0.0.1:${GATEWAY_PORT}/healthz" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    ok "gateway already healthy on :${GATEWAY_PORT} — skipping"
+    EXISTING_PID=$(pgrep -f "openclaw gateway run" | head -1)
+    [ -n "$EXISTING_PID" ] && ok "running PID=$EXISTING_PID"
+else
+    # Reap any stale gateway process.
+    pkill -f "openclaw gateway run" 2>/dev/null || true
+    rm -f "${CONFIG_DIR}/devices/paired.json" \
+          "${CONFIG_DIR}/devices/pending.json" 2>/dev/null || true
+
+    cd "$CONFIG_DIR"
+    # Foreground isn't an option — postStart is a hook Cursor waits on,
+    # blocking it would hang the container start. Plain `&` isn't enough
+    # either: gateway becomes a child of this shell, and when postStart
+    # returns the shell sends SIGHUP and gateway dies within ~5s.
+    # `setsid` puts gateway in a new session (no controlling terminal,
+    # no SIGHUP from postStart's shell exit). Stdin redirected to
+    # /dev/null so it can't be killed via stdin close either.
+    setsid openclaw gateway run --port "${GATEWAY_PORT}" --bind lan --allow-unconfigured --dev \
+        </dev/null > "${LOG}" 2>&1 &
+    GATEWAY_PID=$!
+    ok "gateway:   started PID=${GATEWAY_PID} port=${GATEWAY_PORT}"
+    ok "log:       ${LOG}"
+
+    printf '  %swaiting for /healthz%s' "$DIM" "$RST"
+    READY=0
+    for i in $(seq 1 60); do
+        if ! kill -0 "${GATEWAY_PID}" 2>/dev/null; then
+            printf '\n'
+            fail "gateway exited prematurely after ${i}s"
+            echo "  ${DIM}---- last 40 lines of ${LOG} ----${RST}"
+            tail -40 "${LOG}" | sed "s/^/  ${DIM}/; s/$/${RST}/"
+            echo "  ${DIM}---------------------------------${RST}"
+            exit 1
+        fi
+        if curl -fsS -m 2 "http://127.0.0.1:${GATEWAY_PORT}/healthz" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+            printf ' %sok%s (%ds)\n' "$OK" "$RST" "$i"
+            READY=1
+            break
+        fi
+        printf '.'
+        sleep 1
+    done
+    if [ "${READY}" != "1" ]; then
